title.rendered: Unmissable Events and Experiences Around the World Worth Travelling For

content.rendered:
Some holidays are about slowing down. Others are about being in the right place at exactly the right time. If you’re planning your next trip around the best events around the world to travel for, timing, preparation, and having your travel money sorted can make all the difference.

Around the world, there are moments when destinations come alive, whether that’s through festivals, seasons, or natural wonders. Plan your trip around one of these experiences and you’ll come home with more than photos, you’ll come back with stories.

Wherever you’re heading, getting your travel money sorted before you go means one less thing to think about when you arrive.

Japan Cherry Blossom Season (Sakura)

Japan’s cherry blossom season is one of the most beautiful and eagerly anticipated times of year. For a few short weeks in spring, usually between late March and early April, parks, streets, and temples are filled with delicate pink and white blossoms known as sakura.

Locals gather for hanami picnics beneath the trees, while visitors explore cities like Tokyo and Kyoto at their most photogenic. There’s something quietly special about knowing the blossoms won’t last long, it makes the experience feel fleeting and meaningful.

Spring is also a busy travel period, so planning ahead is essential.

👉 Buy Japanese Yen online before you travel

Cinco de Mayo Celebrations in Mexico

Cinco de Mayo commemorates Mexico’s victory over French forces at the Battle of Puebla in 1862. While it’s often celebrated internationally, the most authentic experience is in Mexico itself, particularly in the city of Puebla.

Festivities include parades, traditional music, historical reenactments, and plenty of street food. It’s lively without being overwhelming and offers a brilliant insight into Mexican culture beyond the usual beach resorts.

Street vendors and smaller bars often prefer cash, so having pesos ready can make exploring much easier.

👉 Buy Mexican Pesos before your trip

Songkran Water Festival in Thailand

Thailand’s New Year celebration, Songkran, is one of the most joyful festivals you’ll ever experience. Held every April, it’s famous for its nationwide water fights, with entire streets turning into friendly battlegrounds of buckets and water guns.

Behind the fun is a symbolic meaning, water represents cleansing and fresh beginnings for the year ahead. Add hot weather, street food, and music, and you have a festival that’s as memorable as it is refreshing.

Small denominations of Thai baht are particularly useful during Songkran.

👉 Buy Thai Baht before you go

Feria de Abril is one of Spain’s most iconic celebrations and a true showcase of Andalusian culture. Held around two weeks after Easter, the week-long fair takes over Seville with flamenco dresses, horse parades, music, dancing, and late nights.

The fairground is filled with colourful casetas where people gather to eat, drink, and dance until the early hours. Even if you’re visiting for the first time, the atmosphere is welcoming and infectious.

During feria week, cash is often preferred at bars, food stalls, and for taxis.

👉 Buy Euros for Spain

Rio Carnival in Brazil

Rio Carnival needs little introduction. Taking place in February or March, it’s one of the world’s most famous festivals and an explosion of colour, rhythm, and energy.

From the spectacular samba parades to the street parties that seem to last around the clock, Carnival is a celebration that pulls you right into the heart of Brazilian culture. It’s loud, busy, and completely unforgettable.

Carrying some Brazilian real alongside your cards is a sensible way to manage spending during Carnival.

👉 Buy Brazilian Real before Carnival

Vienna Christmas Markets, Austria

Vienna is beautiful year-round, but in the run-up to Christmas it becomes something truly special. From mid-November onwards, the city’s squares fill with traditional Christmas markets selling handmade gifts, festive treats, and warming mugs of mulled wine.

The market at Rathausplatz is one of the most well-known, set against the grand backdrop of Vienna’s City Hall. It’s ideal for a winter city break filled with festive atmosphere and old-world charm.

Many stalls are cash-only, particularly for food and drink.

👉 Buy Euros for Austria

Northern Lights in Iceland

For something quieter but no less special, seeing the Northern Lights in Iceland is an experience that stays with you. Best viewed between September and March, the aurora borealis lights up the night sky in shifting shades of green and purple.

It’s unpredictable, which somehow makes it even more rewarding when it happens. Combine it with Iceland’s dramatic landscapes, hot springs, and cosy cafés, and you have a truly unique trip.

Some tours and rural locations still rely on local payments, so having Icelandic krona can be useful.

👉 Buy Icelandic Krona before you travel

Plan the Trip, Sort the Currency

Travelling for a special event or season takes a bit of planning, and your travel money should be part of that plan. Ordering currency in advance can mean better exchange rates, fewer surprises, and more time to enjoy the experience once you arrive.

From cherry blossoms and flamenco dancing to Christmas lights and natural wonders, wherever you’re going next, make sure your money is ready to travel too.

✈️ Order your travel money online with Travel FX

@graph.name: Lucy Reed
@graph.description: Hi! I’m Lucy, as well as writing blog posts I lead Customer Experience and UX at Travel FX. With over 15 years’ experience in the foreign exchange industry, I specialise in helping UK travellers understand exchange rates, avoid unnecessary fees, and feel confident about their travel money before they fly. Having lived both in the UK and overseas, and travelled extensively over the years, I understand first-hand how important it is to get the details right when you’re planning a trip. From managing your travel budget to choosing the right foreign currency or travel money card, small decisions can make a big difference.
